summary: Proved driver-native GBM DMA-BUF allocation on the advertised Hyprland capture device. The throwaway direct Wayland client allocated an NVIDIA block-linear AR24 buffer on /dev/dri/renderD128, imported it through zwp_linux_dmabuf_v1, submitted it to ext_image_copy_capture_frame_v1, and received a ready frame with timestamp, transform, and full damage.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: Use a node-scoped generated Hyprland output with cleanup evidence.
Record allocator dependencies and whether the Nix dev shell needs native allocation dependencies.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: nix-develop-dependencies.txt records missing pkg-config in the repo shell; notes.md recommends pkg-config, wayland-scanner, wayland, wayland-protocols, libgbm, and libdrm.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: Allocate a DMA-BUF against the advertised capture device or justified same-driver device using an advertised format/modifier when possible.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: native-dmabuf-capture.json records advertised /dev/dri/renderD128, GBM backend nvidia, AR24, modifier 216172782120099856, stride 7680, and one plane.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: Submit the buffer through zwp_linux_dmabuf_v1 to ext_image_copy_capture_frame_v1.
Evidence includes advertised device, allocation node/device, selected format, modifier, stride/planes, timestamp/damage, ready/fail result, and cleanup.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: validation-summary.json and native-dmabuf-capture.json include the required fields; cleanup is in hyprland-monitors-after-remove.json.

commandsRun.command: plain PATH, nix develop, and temporary nix-shell dependency probes
wayland-info filtered to direct capture and linux-dmabuf globals
focused jq JSON validation, redaction scan, line check, and nix develop -c just verify

result.allocatorCondition: GBM on advertised render node with explicit NVIDIA block-linear modifier succeeds.
result.previousFailureDisposition: The prior dumb-buffer PRIME frame.failed was allocator-specific.
residualNotes: The proof helper is disposable evidence code, not a committed production capture implementation.
The current repo dev shell cannot rebuild this helper without adding native Wayland/GBM development dependencies.
